title: Complex Frequency and the Exponential Function
summary: This page introduces the complex exponential function e^(st) where s = σ + jω is the complex frequency, showing it as a generalization of Euler's formula. It demonstrates that e^(st) encompasses constants, monotonic exponentials, sinusoids, and exponentially varying sinusoids as special cases. The complex frequency plane (s-plane) is introduced with real (σ) and imaginary (ω) axes.

figures.caption: Sinusoids of complex frequency σ + jω showing four cases: (a) pure real exponentials for σ > 0, σ = 0, and σ < 0; (b) undamped sinusoid for σ = 0; (c) decaying sinusoid for σ < 0; and (d) growing sinusoid for σ > 0.
figures.description: The figure has four subplots with horizontal time axis t and waveform amplitude versus time, illustrating signals associated with complex frequency s = σ + jω. Panel (a) plots e^{σt} for σ>0, σ=0, and σ<0, showing exponential growth, a constant line, and exponential decay; panel (b) shows an undamped sinusoid labeled σ=0. Panels (c) and (d) show sinusoids bounded by dashed exponential envelopes, with σ<0 producing a decaying sinusoid and σ>0 producing a growing sinusoid. Students should notice that σ controls whether amplitude decays, stays constant, or grows, while ω gives the oscillation in the sinusoidal cases.
